Data Processing and MAR Control Addendum — Valdora Industrials SE (EU issuer) for operations on the Nordhaven Growth MTF, supervised by the Pan‑EU Markets Supervisory Office (PMSO).
Purpose: processing for share listing, investor relations, CSRD sustainability metrics, and SFDR disclosures, plus maintenance of insider lists and trade monitoring; legal bases include GDPR Art 6(1)(b),(f) and Art 9(2)(b),(g) where special categories appear in whistleblowing or works‑council files.
Identifiability example cluster: Ms. Elin Jorvik (born 1988-04-13), Sustainability Reporting Lead at Valdora Industrials SE, office location Kepler Quay 11, 3rd District, Nordhaven EU‑27; work email elin.jorvik@valdora-se.example; device tag VAL-SE-AX9C-2048 with MDM profile “VAL-CSR-44”; cookie mar_session=NORD-8844-ELJ; building badge S/N NB-302771; support ticket ACC-GL-4472; taken together these quasi‑identifiers render her identifiable under GDPR.
We also log contextual telemetry such as client device locale “nb-NH”, truncated IP 10.14.5.22, and Bluetooth hash BE:12:34:56:78:9A for access control; when stored alone and salted, these are treated as pseudonymous but still personal data.
Negative illustration: descriptions such as “a 34‑year‑old analyst in a large EU capital using a mid‑range smartphone” or “two interns on a 3‑month contract” are not used alone to single out any person and should not be relied upon for identification.
MNPI handling: under EU MAR, any precise non‑public information about Valdora Industrials SE or its securities that would be likely to significantly affect price is classified as inside information and stored only in the “MAR Vault” with role‑based access and tamper‑evident audit trails.
Insider list workflow: creation on trigger events (e.g., draft green bond framework, undisclosed CSRD metrics variance), validation by Compliance, notification via secure channel, and filing to PMSO upon request; retention for 5 years from the end of the event per MAR.
Special category data (GDPR Art 9) are not collected for routine HR or investor workflows; however, grievance notes may incidentally include trade‑union membership or health details, which are minimised, access‑controlled, and subject to stricter retention (max 2 years) and redaction before transfers.
Cross‑border transfers: processors Seafrost Cloud Ltd. (Aurelia) and Orimex Data S.A. (Sundora) receive limited datasets under SCCs with supplementary encryption at rest and in transit; Transfer Impact Assessment IDs TR-VAL-2026-07 and TR-VAL-2026-09 record residual risk and safeguards per GDPR Art 46.
We do not sell personal data, we do not use biometric templates for authentication, and generic demographic statistics (e.g., 52% employees aged 25–45) are published in aggregate for CSRD without identifying individuals.
Data subject rights: access, rectification, erasure, restriction, portability, and objection may be exercised via privacy.office@valdora-se.example or the DPO at dpo@valdora-se.example; postal contact: Data Office, Kepler Quay 11, 3rd District, Nordhaven EU‑27.
Trade surveillance data (order IDs, timestamps, and pseudonymous trader codes) are processed to meet market‑abuse prevention obligations; note that “order code 7Q-ALFA” alone does not identify a natural person without additional keys kept by Compliance.
Security controls: AES‑256 encryption, key rotation every 90 days, privileged access reviews, and device compliance checks (e.g., laptop VAL-SE-AX9C-2048 must pass patch level NH‑2026.06) with alerts to secops@valdora-se.example.
Retention: CSRD/SFDR source records are kept 10 years; investor relation inquiries 3 years; MAR Vault artifacts 5 years post‑publication; data that are not required for regulatory reporting are anonymised or deleted according to schedule VAL‑RS‑12.
